notes: This document governs how all engineering decisions, outputs, and parameter changes must be structured. Treat this as a contract between Antigravity AI and the product team.

S1_machine_readable_output.description: All balancing artifacts must be emitted as JSON with a 'notes' field. Human-readable copy may accompany but the JSON artifact is authoritative.
S1_machine_readable_output.enforcement: CI lint step checks for presence of required_fields in any JSON under /patches/ and /economy/
S1_machine_readable_output.default_en: All game config changes must ship as machine-readable JSON artifacts.

S2_explain_tunables.description: Every formula that ships in game_config.json must expose exactly 3 tunable knobs, each with: current value, safe_range [min, max], provenance string, and notes string.
S2_explain_tunables.enforcement: Automated schema validator runs on CI against game_config.json
S2_explain_tunables.default_en: Every formula must document 3 tunable knobs with safe ranges.

S3_no_magic_numbers.description: Every numeric constant introduced in code or config must either reference a named tunable in game_config.json or include an inline comment with provenance. Anonymous literals in game logic are a build warning.
S3_no_magic_numbers.enforcement: ESLint custom rule: no-magic-numbers (configured with exceptions list)
S3_no_magic_numbers.default_en: No anonymous numeric constants. All must be named with provenance.

S4_simulation_first.description: Any proposed change to a formula tunable must be validated by the Monte Carlo balance simulator before being accepted into game_config.json. The simulation must run at least 1000 virtual-player sessions.
S4_simulation_first.enforcement: simulation/balance_sim.ts must be re-run and output appended to simulation/reports/ before merging any economy change.
S4_simulation_first.default_en: All balance changes must be validated by the Monte Carlo simulation first.

S5_rollback_metadata.description: Every parameter patch must be stored as a JSON file in /patches/ with the required rollback schema.

patch_schema.rollback_conditions: array of strings describing when to revert
patch_schema.sim_report_ref: path to simulation output that validated this patch
patch_schema.approved_by: string (product lead name or 'auto' if within safe_range)

S5_rollback_metadata.enforcement: CI block: patches without all schema fields will not be applied to production config.
S5_rollback_metadata.default_en: Every parameter patch must include rollback conditions and simulation evidence.

S6_localization_keys.description: Every player-facing string must have a loc_key and a default 'en' string in src/i18n/en.json. Hardcoded strings in UI components are a lint error.
S6_localization_keys.enforcement: ESLint rule: no-hardcoded-ui-strings (custom). CI runs i18n-checker to ensure all loc_keys in en.json are used.
S6_localization_keys.default_en: All player-facing text must use loc_keys from the i18n system.

S7_security_and_fairness.description: The game must not implement pay-to-win mechanics. Gems (premium currency) are restricted to cosmetic and quality-of-life purchases only. Any proposed feature that gives paying players a numeric gameplay advantage must be rejected.
S7_security_and_fairness.pay_to_win_definition: Any purchase that increases coins, CPS, crop yield, machine efficiency, or prestige points beyond what is achievable by free players within the same time window.
allowed_gem_purchases: Offline income time extension (max 4h, matches free 4h cap)
forbidden_gem_purchases: Crop yield boosters beyond free player ceiling
Exclusive machines or workers not earnable by free players
Skip timers that reduce growth time beyond the free cap
S7_security_and_fairness.enforcement: Code review gate: any new GemShop item must be listed in allowed_gem_purchases. Automated scan for 'coins += gem' patterns.
S7_security_and_fairness.default_en: No pay-to-win. Gems are for cosmetics and convenience only.

team_roles.notes: Roles and their responsibilities when working with Antigravity.
team_roles.product_manager: Accepts/rejects features and sets acceptance criteria. Approves patches flagged as manual-review.
team_roles.data_scientist: Validates simulation outputs for statistical significance. Signs off on S4 reports.
team_roles.backend_engineer: Implements APIs and database schemas. Ensures all tables include version and patch_id columns.
team_roles.frontend_engineer: Implements text UI and state management. Enforces S6 loc_key discipline.
team_roles.qa_engineer: Runs exploit-hunting and fuzz sessions. Files rollback_conditions when vulnerabilities discovered.
team_roles.live_ops_manager: Plans campaigns and coordinates rollbacks. Has authority to invoke rollback_conditions unilaterally.
team_roles.antigravity_ai: Designs, generates, simulates, and documents. Does not unilaterally deploy to production.
